item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Author here: thanks for doing this. I wrote this years ago when I didn't think about things like copyright and recently meant to come back and take it down, but it slipped my mind.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image belongs to John Isaac. As there is no indication it was ever released under CC 3.0, it is assumed copyrighted and thus has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the transition condition from -B to -C to be immensely interesting. That part is awesome. I'm not sold at all on the rest, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly wish I had got to this scip before the rewrite. Having read the original and compared it to the current version I think the current one lacks a lot of punch. It went from a dangerous pathogen that actively seeks to infect new hosts once activated (strongly reminiscent of some real parasites) to it's a thing that turns your bones and muscles to liquid. The containment procedures also seem excessive for the current version as well as it no longer tries to infect staff after conversion. Oh and "Hypothetically" in the third paragraph is missing a t. Going to go fix it now.
Edit: fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Knew I was forgetting something. Thanks for the heads up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture shows the designations as -2 and -3, instead of -B and -C

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There. Removed a lot of the LOLEDGY crap that was making this thing a massive personal embarrassment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I need to go back and re-write this some day. I feel like my writing has improved a lot since I wrote this (or maybe I'm just more self-aware and hate myself more) and I think it can be improved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Worst. Superpower. Ever.

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing might be standard, just a disease like 008 or 217. However, that was an excellent choice of picture. It added a nice WTF factor the the article. Upvote for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Picture isn't loading for me.

e: Loads now, problem was on my end

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it quite simply describes that it's a microorganism that turns you to goo, I don't really see how you didn't get that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1332 is a pathogen suspected to be artificial in nature, though a source has yet to be accurately determined.

Is it a bacteria, fungus, protozoa, prion, or what? If it's not any of those, but some sort of weird new infectious agent, then how did they culture it, isolate it and purify it to get the culture state samples?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this article doesn't quite stand on its own without the picture—if I couldn't see the image, I'm not sure I'd understand what was going on from the text. However, I felt that the tone and style were generally pretty good. No vote for now.

item.title: Lovin this
item.content:encoded:
I love this! It's wonderfully creepy
though I do believe the addendum is unneeded. The whole transformation and the potential psychological horror of it all is wonderful enough to support it without the cliched need for someone to be violent when infected with a virus.

Besides wouldn't lashing out with spines and tentacles be a physical action outside the norm of the infectee's physical activity? Violent action would in most cases cause the death of the subject.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was interesting until Resident Evil arm. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a bit meh on the concept, but the execution is convoluted enough to downvote. The differentiation between SCP-1332-1, 2, and 3 in text is confusing and the temperament change is blandly delivered and feels tacked-on. I dislike viral SCPs in general, and to make a good body horror concept work you need to make it feel personally resonant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Theoretically, it is possible for specimens of SCP-1332-2 to live fully normal lives without ever becoming aware of their condition. Research staff are expressly prohibited from testing this theory.

That's a hypothesis, not a theory.

item.title: So...
item.content:encoded: Why is he naked? Also, excellent work. I would upvote twice if I could

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing is quite nice, and that is an excellent picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad, all things considered. You get a tad lolfoundation with "Research staff are expressly prohibited from testing this theory." and I felt like some parts of the description were actually containment. But if nothing else, I upvoted for good use of that picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Curious… the imagery of the -3's lashing out with metallic pseudopods made of their own bones and muscles is oddly appealing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was pretty excited about reading this when I saw the picture.
Kind of gave me high expectations, you know?

I can't say I'm impressed with the effect. Kind of feels like you found an awesome picture and created a mediocre SCP to attach to it.

There's a lot of explanation, but not a lot of "implication". There's no hook to it. There's no drama, no mystery and no horror. I'm sure you could modify this to have those things, but right now the article doesnt have them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has a lot of potential, but I agree that they shouldn't become some sort of violent zombie. Keep them tragic.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I changed a little bit about the violent behavior to explain it a little more. It's not longer just random "panic and kill everything near me"; now they attack with purpose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was with you until I got to the sudden violent behavior change- it feels tacked on and doesn't really add much. The unusual strategies the -3 instances use to escape are worth keeping, but surely there are more interesting motivations for them than arbitrary violence…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it. However, your redactions are unnecessary, improper, and distracting. Nevertheless, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dislike it because I think the redacteds are sloppy and there is a lot of comma spam (or at least I found there was a lot of comma spam) throughout the article.

The concept itself (virus that make you… control every piece of your body either consciously or unconsciously?) might be fine. But executed here I found boring. Also the random violence. I don't understand why there is random violence eeking about.

suspected to be artificial in nature, though a source has yet to be accurately determined

The fact that you mention this but then never look back to it makes it kind of flop onto the floor like a dead fish, making the whole room stink with its rot if not attended to. There's no other build up or ways it can add to the article, it's just there. This same issue is one I have with the randomly tacked-on-seeming violence.

Research staff are expressly prohibited from testing this theory.

Ditto here. I also wouldn't know why the heck not, since as far as I can read the thing only infects others when it injures others. Unless I got this wrong, since I'm very much tired.

Overall, meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, if only for the concept. +1 get.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first SCP. I hope it doesn't blow too bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm Djoric and what is this.
